title: Hoggon
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Character |type= |image=[[File:Hoggon.jpg]] |name=Hoggon |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]][[Tales of the Jedi – Redemption 1|''Tales of the Jedi – Redemption'' 1]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Brown]] |feathers= |eyes=Brown |skin=Light |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation= |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|I just love watching the Jedi. Don't you?|Hoggon|}} '''Hoggon''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Spacer/Legends|spacer]] of short stature who was fascinated with the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] and its history. In [[3986 BBY]], he unwittingly helped war criminal and former [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]] find a suitable world of exile—at first he suggested [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]], but Qel-Droma chose to settle on [[Rhen Var/Legends|Rhen Var]]. Immediately after delivering his client to his new home, Hoggon went to [[Exis Station/Legends|Exis Station]], to witness the [[Conclave on Exis Station|Jedi conclave]] that was taking place there. As he departed, he discovered that [[Vima Sunrider]] had been stowing away on his [[Hoggon's starship|ship]]. The young girl, daughter of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Nomi Sunrider]], sought Qel-Droma's teachings, and when she showed a [[Hologram/Legends|hologram]] of the man to Hoggon, he realized that it was the very same person he had taken to Rhen Var. Delivering the young girl to Rhen Var, Hoggon then decided to inform the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] of Qel-Droma's location, hoping that he would be privy to a battle between them and the exile. At first he took his information to Nomi Sunrider, but then chose to inform the [[Cathar/Legends|Cathar]] Jedi [[Sylvar]], who was highly critical of Qel-Droma. Taking Sylvar to Rhen Var, Hoggon watched on as she [[Duel on Rhen Var|battled]] the exile. However, Sylvar was unable to kill Qel-Droma, and, seeing his opportunity to enter the history books, Hoggon shot Qel-Droma instead. As Hoggon had hoped, his deed was in fact included in historical documents written thousands of years later. ==Biography== ===The unidentified stranger=== {{Quote|Great events happened here on Yavin 4…you can get a real sense of history. Those are the temples he built…can't you just imagine the ships in the air? It gives me shivers to be here…I can almost feel the echos…|Hoggon, speaking to his employer during their voyage to Yavin 4|}} [[File:Hoggonship1.jpg|thumb|left|Hoggon's starship.]] By [[3986 BBY]], the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] Hoggon was [[Captain/Legends|captain]] of [[Hoggon's starship|his own starship]], although he was not in the best of situations financially.{{DB|character|ulicqeldroma|Qel-Droma, Ulic}} He took a particular interest in the exploits of the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], particularly the events that had made up the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]]. During that year, Hoggon was hired by an [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|unidentified man]] to find a suitable place of solitude in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. Hoggon obliged, and first took his employer to [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]], site of a [[Battle of Yavin 4 (Great Sith War)|decisive battle]] in the waning days of the Great Sith War. Landing on the [[Moon/Legends|moon's]] surface, Hoggon was anxious to attend the [[Conclave on Exis Station]] taking place elsewhere—a large meeting of [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] from all over the galaxy, and so he tried to sell Yavin 4's suitability to his client as quickly as possible. The pair explored the [[Temple of Exar Kun]] as Hoggon told the story of how [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] Ulic Qel-Droma had betrayed his compatriot, [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]], at the Battle of Yavin 4, leading to Kun's final defeat. The client was dissatisfied with the location, however, and Hoggon was forced to look for a new candidate. His next choice was [[Rhen Var/Legends|Rhen Var]]—a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] going through its ice age, and as a result, devoid of civilization. As he brought his [[Starship/Legends|ship]] into land, Hoggon narrowly avoided being crushed by a large block of ice falling from a nearby cliff side. Hoggon then showed his client some of Rhen Var's ruins—the taciturn man decided that Rhen Var was suitable for his purposes. Hoggon was pleased, as he would then be able to attend the Conclave on [[Exis Station/Legends|Exis Station]]. ===Conclave=== {{Quote|I'm anxious to finish this assignment so I can head off to Exis Station. Did I tell you? Nomi Sunrider called a great Jedi convocation, and I want to be able to say I was there.|Hoggon|}} Traveling to Exis Station, Hoggon had to pay premium berthing rates on arrival. Debarking from his ship, he met with an old friend of his, [[Dakar (port administrator)|Dakar]], who, when paid, tipped him off as to a good location from which he could observe the conclave: an old air shaft overlooking the gathering hall. Making his way there, Hoggon watched on as the [[Cathar/Legends|Cathar]] Jedi [[Sylvar]] passionately spoke out against the exiled former Sith Lord and Jedi Knight Ulic Qel-Droma. When the conclave was over, Hoggon departed on his ship, but soon discovered that young [[Vima Sunrider]], daughter of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Master]] [[Nomi Sunrider]], had stowed away onboard. The young girl hoped to find Qel-Droma, and presented Hoggon with a [[Hologram/Legends|hologram]] of him—to the [[Spacer/Legends|spacer's]] surprise, it was the very same man he had taken to Rhen Var.[[Tales of the Jedi – Redemption 2|''Tales of the Jedi – Redemption'' 2]] The young Sunrider offered to pay Hoggon well if he were to take her to Qel-Droma, and he obliged. Hoggon gave the young girl proper supplies and gear for traversing Rhen Var's frozen plains, and also gave her a [[Transmitter/Legends|transmitter]], should she wish to contact him, before bidding her farewell. Several days later, while repairing his ship's [[Autopilot/Legends|autopilot]], Hoggon began obsessing over the fact that his craft had played host to not only Ulic Qel-Droma, but Vima Sunrider. He deduced that Nomi Sunrider would not be particularly pleased with the developments, and that one of the Jedi should be told what had happened to the two. Musing over which particular Jedi to tell, Hoggon excitedly supposed that they would sent a [[Battalion/Legends|battalion]] of Jedi Knights to Rhen Var to battle Qel-Droma. He then resolved to return to Exis Station, to tell the elder Sunrider of the development.[[Tales of the Jedi – Redemption 3|''Tales of the Jedi – Redemption'' 3]] ==="Hoggon the Great"=== {{Quote|Hoggon the Great! History will–''"
"''If not for the man you just killed, I'd be tearing your throat out right now.''"
"''I guess I'll never understand Jedi…|Hoggon and Sylvar, just after Hoggon killed Ulic Qel-Droma|[[Tales of the Jedi – Redemption 5|''Tales of the Jedi – Redemption'' 5]]}} [[File:SylvarAndHoggon-TotJR5.jpg|thumb|150px|Sylvar grabs Hoggon immediately after he shoots Qel-Droma.]] Back at the station, however, Hoggon was refused audience by the Jedi Master, who had already learned of Vima's situation. Hoggon protested loudly as he was removed from Sunrider's presence, to no avail. Realizing that he needed to find another Jedi to inform, he remembered how Sylvar had railed against Qel-Droma during the conclave. The Cathar Jedi returned to Exis Station shortly afterward, and Hoggon approached her with his information. Angered, she picked the diminutive spacer up off the ground, but relented when she realized that he knew where Qel-Droma was. Hoggon then offered to take her to Rhen Var personally, and she obliged.[[Tales of the Jedi – Redemption 4|''Tales of the Jedi – Redemption'' 4]] Returning to Rhen Var once more, Hoggon chose to use an angle of approach to the ruins that he hadn't used before, to catch Qel-Droma off guard. Collecting his [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]], the spacer offered to help Sylvar battle Qel-Droma, but the Jedi declined the offer. She then debarked, and [[Duel on Rhen Var|engaged]] the former Sith Lord in a duel, while Hoggon watched on, being particularly excited when Sylvar backed Qel-Droma up against a cliff. However, Qel-Droma, who had been trying to atone for his previous [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] transgressions, refused to fight her, and Sylvar found herself unable to strike him down. As she deactivated her [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] blade, Hoggon took it upon himself to shoot Qel-Droma through the chest. Sylvar turned on the spacer in a rage, but let him go free, since Qel-Droma's [[Death/Legends|death]] had helped free her from the anger that had consumed her since the Sith War. Confused, Hoggon departed the scene as Qel-Droma died in Sunrider's arms. Just as he had hoped, Hoggon made his mark on history for his deed. In [[36 ABY]], Hoggon was mentioned in the writings of [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] historian [[Voren Na'al/Legends|Voren Na'al]], as the man who shot Ulic Qel-Droma.''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' ==Personality and traits== Hoggon was fascinated with Jedi culture and history—despite not having been there, he was very familiar with how the Battle of Yavin 4, during the Great Sith War, had played out in years prior. The spacer was so engrossed with the Jedi that he was happy enough just to watch them, and was overjoyed when he met one in person. He also believed that he needed to be an important figure in Jedi history, and went out of his way to make sure that he was so. This belief led him to kill an unarmed Ulic Qel-Droma, although all he received from the Jedi for doing so was ire and disgust. In his eyes, he was a hero for having done so, and the act was a noble one. He was also an enterprising being, but not an inconsiderate one, taking measures to make sure Vima Sunrider was well-equipped when she debarked to track Rhen Var's wastelands. ==Behind the scenes== Hoggon made his first appearance in [[Kevin J. Anderson]]'s [[1998]] miniseries [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – Redemption|''Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi - Redemption'']]—specifically, [[Tales of the Jedi – Redemption 1|''Tales of the Jedi – Redemption'' 1]]. In the story, he played a supporting role. ==Appearances== *[[Tales of the Jedi – Redemption 1|''Tales of the Jedi – Redemption'' 1]] {{1st}} *[[Tales of the Jedi – Redemption 2|''Tales of the Jedi – Redemption'' 2]] *[[Tales of the Jedi – Redemption 3|''Tales of the Jedi – Redemption'' 3]] *[[Tales of the Jedi – Redemption 4|''Tales of the Jedi – Redemption'' 4]] *[[Tales of the Jedi – Redemption 5|''Tales of the Jedi – Redemption'' 5]] *''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' {{Codex}} ==Sources== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Hoggon}} *[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']] *''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' *''[[The Dark Side Sourcebook]]'' {{Imo}} *''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' {{Imo}} *''[[Power of the Jedi Sourcebook]]'' *[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: The Clone Wars'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]] *{{FactFile|113|QEL 1-2|Redemption of Ulic Qel-Droma}} *''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' *''[[Star Wars: The Comics Companion]]'' *[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']] *''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'' {{Imo}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' {{Imo}} *{{DB|character|ulicqeldroma|Qel-Droma, Ulic}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Hoggon |es=Hoggon |pl=Hoggon |fi=Hoggon }} [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Spacers]]
